name: Crown of Inanna
description: The Crown of Inanna, also known as the Queen of Heaven's crown, is a potent symbol of Inanna's power, authority, and divine status in Sumerian mythology. It represents her dominion over love, beauty, fertility, war, and political power. The crown is often described as a towering headdress adorned with precious stones, notably lapis lazuli and carnelian, and sometimes features golden horns or feathers, signifying her connection to both the earthly and celestial realms. It is believed to amplify her inherent abilities, granting her unmatched influence over the fate of mortals and even the other gods. The crown was not merely an ornamental object; it was integral to Inanna's ability to enact her will and maintain cosmic balance. Its loss or theft would signify a disruption in the natural order and a weakening of Inanna's influence. The crown is a visual representation of the divine decrees (ME) that Inanna controlled, allowing her to shape civilization and enforce her edicts. The myths surrounding the crown often involve quests to obtain or protect it, highlighting its significance as a source of power and a symbol of legitimate rule. It is a crucial element in understanding Inanna's complex and multifaceted role in the Sumerian pantheon and her enduring legacy as a powerful and influential goddess. Without her crown, Inanna is diminished, losing access to her divine powers and the ability to maintain order in the cosmos. Possessing the crown meant possessing a portion of Inanna's very essence.
subtitle: Symbol of Inanna's divine power
origin: Forged by the gods as a symbol of Inanna's dominion
currentLocation: Unknown, possibly lost or hidden in the underworld

keyMyths.title: Inanna's Descent to the Underworld
keyMyths.description: Inanna removes her crown, among other garments, as she descends into the underworld, relinquishing her power. This signifies her vulnerability and the stripping away of her divine status.

keyMyths.title: Inanna and Enki
keyMyths.description: In some versions, Inanna obtains the ME, the divine decrees, through trickery from Enki. The crown is a symbol of her possession and control over these decrees.
keyMyths.source: Inanna and Enki: The Transfer of the Arts of Civilization

sources.author: Diane Wolkstein
sources.work: Inanna, Queen of Heaven and Earth
sources.description: A compilation and translation of Sumerian myths about Inanna.
